title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Main
December is drawing to a close, with the large vessel classes signalling an upward trend in rates, while the supramax and handysize vessel classes remain in the doldrums. Demand growth in tonne days (see chart below) indicates a stable final month this year, as growth exceeded last year's million tonne days for a similar period. It remains to be seen how Chinese economic activity and iron ore demand will develop in the final quarter following the zero interest rate measures.

Steady strength of Chinese iron ore imports, 64% comes from Australia
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Demand Ton Charts, Capesize & Panamax
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-reports-toncharts/2ly571/293077710?h=3WmQ0W2HieYt9Yq_GjPDi6GgoQNDoaIyKRfHuDhLsGo

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) Weaker
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
The third week of December signaled an upswing in the large vessel categories, while slowing momentum in the supramax and handysize segments remained steady since late November. Capesize vessel freight rates rose to $21/tonne, up $3/tonne from the week 47 level, while the last peak was $20/tonne 6 weeks ago.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - Ballasters (# vessels) Increasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
There is an upward trend in all vessel size categories except Capesize, while the number of ballast vessels in the Handysize and Supramax size categories has been above the annual average for the past eight weeks. Capesize SE Africa: The number of vessels now stands at 58, 22 fewer than two weeks ago and below the annual average for the past three weeks.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND - TonDays Increasing
children.summary: Capesize demand growth has accelerated significantly since the end of week 48, while Panamax demand has gradually increased. Capesize demand ton-days: The upward trend of the last three weeks supported the rebound in freight rates in early December, which continued through the end of the month.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing
section_path: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing
children.section_path_text: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
In the last days of December, congestion approached the last peak of week 27, with a continuous increase in the Supramax, and Handysize segments. Capesize: The number of vessels increased to 132, 17 more than in week 49.
